cross_scene_arcs.title: The Empathetic Guardian: From Rescue to Protection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bill's core motivation evolves from rescuing animals and people to becoming the central protector against an escalating biological threat, consistently prioritizing empathy and preservation of life even in the face of horror.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bill uses compassion to rescue a trapped deer, standing apart from his peers.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bill actively fights to protect the group using improvised weapons, fully embracing his role as defender.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His empathetic nature is tested by the necessity of violence, raising questions about whether compassion can survive in a world overrun by horror.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Love and Rivalry: The Fractured Romance with Starla
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bill's emotional connection with Starla grows through intimate moments and shared trauma, but is continually undermined by Grant's presence and ultimately destroyed by betrayal and fatal mistrust.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bill shares a tender, poetic moment with Starla, creating emotional intimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Starla kills Bill, ending their relationship in tragedy and ambiguity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains unclear whether Starla's final act was self-defense, retribution, or a misjudgment, leaving their love story unresolved and haunting.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Mocked Outsider to Reluctant Leader
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bill transitions from being dismissed by his peers to earning leadership through insight and moral courage, only to lose it through failure and capture as the crisis overwhelms human hierarchies.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bill's compassionate approach is mocked, though successful.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bill is physically subdued and dominated by Grant, stripped of all authority and agency.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His rise and fall as a leader reflect the collapse of order, but whether his leadership could have succeeded under different circumstances remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Unraveling Investigation: From Clarity to Chaos
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bill's methodical investigation into Brenda's disappearance spirals into a confrontation with an incomprehensible parasite outbreak, transforming a criminal inquiry into a fight for survival against a supernatural threat.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bill builds a logical case implicating Grant in Brenda's disappearance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bill is overpowered by Grant's monstrous transformation, his investigative framework shattered by the supernatural.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The truth behind the outbreak and whether justice is possible in a world where reality itself is corrupted remains unanswered.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Descent and Final Reckoning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bill's journey from self-sacrifice to moral crisis culminates in his death at Starla's hands, raising questions about whether his violent instincts ultimately undermined his humanity despite his protective intentions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bill chooses to return to danger to save his friends, sacrificing escape for loyalty.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bill is shot dead by Starla, possibly due to his own escalating aggression and inability to escape his nature.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Bill's death was inevitable due to his inner violence or a tragic misunderstanding remains morally ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Control: From Authority to Possession
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Grant's relationship with Starla deteriorates from emotionally abusive control to outright psychological domination, revealing his deepening need to assert ownership over her as his sense of self unravels.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grant verbally dominates Starla over a professional call, asserting control under the guise of protection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Grant restricts access to parts of the house and psychologically intimidates Starla, treating her as property to be contained.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Starla's autonomy remains suppressed, and the relationship becomes increasingly one-sided and dangerous, foreshadowing physical escalation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Infection and Transformation: The Loss of Humanity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Grant's physical and psychological transformation from a controlled, authoritative man to a monstrous vector of alien infection unfolds through escalating biological and behavioral changes, culminating in total loss of human identity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grant is infected by an alien spore that embeds in his cerebellum, initiating neurological and physical changes.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Grant fully transforms into a slug-like creature, no longer recognizable as human and entirely alienated from society.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains unclear whether any remnant of Grant's consciousness survives within the creature, leaving moral ambiguity about his culpability.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Meat Philosophy: Descent into Primal Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Grant abandons human norms and embraces a dehumanizing, cannibalistic worldview centered on meat as both sustenance and identity, reflecting his psychological regression and alien-driven transformation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grant impulsively buys all available meat, signaling compulsive behavior and a need for control through excess.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Grant feeds rotting, maggot-infested meat to a captive Brenda and hides meat in secret, fully embracing a grotesque, animalistic existence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The symbolic and literal extension of the 'you're meat' philosophy to humans—including potential self-cannibalism or consumption of Starla—remains unexplored but implied.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Reconnection to Violent Possession
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Grant's emotional fixation on Starla evolves from idealized affection to obsessive, violent compulsion, culminating in an attempt to forcibly transform her, revealing love corrupted by alien influence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grant experiences emotional awakening and reverent fixation on Starla, seeking reconnection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Grant attacks Starla to infect her, and is ultimately killed by her in self-defense, marking the tragic collapse of their relationship.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Grant's final actions were driven by love, biological imperative, or both remains ambiguous, complicating his moral legacy.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Unseen Crisis: From Denial to Global Threat
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Grant transitions from an oblivious authority figure during the onset of a global crisis to the central vector of an alien invasion, ultimately becoming an unstoppable, inhuman force before his destruction.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grant dismisses the meteor-induced tremor, maintaining routine and control as the crisis begins.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Grant becomes invulnerable and supernaturally powerful, then explodes violently, ending his role in the escalating threat.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full scope of the alien threat beyond Grant—and whether it continues after his death—remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fracture and Reconciliation of Trust
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Starla's relationship with Grant fractures when she resists his advances, triggering his anger, but is temporarily mended when she accepts his apology and re-engages emotionally and physically, symbolically reenacting their wedding day.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Starla asserts personal boundaries by rejecting unwanted intimacy, creating emotional distance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She abandons her resistance and embraces vulnerability, reaffirming her commitment to Grant.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The mysterious wound on Grant's abdomen undermines the authenticity of the reconciliation, foreshadowing deeper betrayal.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Autonomy and Rise of Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Starla's status within her home and marriage deteriorates from hopeful partner to locked-out outsider, culminating in her forced discovery of Grant's pet-murder operation, marking the collapse of domestic safety and her transition into investigator.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Starla hopes for marital stability and return to normalcy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is physically and emotionally excluded, then becomes a traumatized witness to horrific crimes hidden in her own home.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The true extent of Grant's control and the nature of the 'birthday present' remain veiled, deepening psychological unease.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Survivor to Sacrificial Agent
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Starla evolves from a passive survivor seeking escape to an active agent who sacrifices herself to stop Grant, using her emotional bond as a weapon and ultimately detonating a grenade to end his threat.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Starla seeks safety and autonomy, breaking from Bill's authority to act independently.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She willingly becomes bait, is captured, and chooses self-destruction to stop Grant, completing her transformation into a self-sacrificial figure.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether her sacrifice leads to redemption or is in vain remains unknown, as does the impact on the larger conflict.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Monstrous Bond
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Starla attempts to reforge her bond with Grant by offering Bill as a sacrifice, achieving temporary emotional breakthrough before being physically incapacitated and ultimately choosing to destroy him.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Starla seeks belonging by aligning herself fully with Grant, offering Bill as proof of loyalty.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Grant briefly accepts her, but she ultimately kills him to end his monstrous existence, severing their bond permanently.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her suppressed disgust and psychological cost of devotion suggest the relationship was never truly healed, only manipulated.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Breaking Cycles of Trauma and Reclaiming Agency
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Starla confronts her past abuse, survives escalating violence, kills to protect herself, executes Bill to reclaim control, and ultimately drives away alone—symbolizing her break from victimhood and dependency.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: She reveals her traumatic past and reliance on others for protection, still defined by cycles of violence.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She acts decisively, kills Bill with resolve, and takes full control of her future, driving away independently.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional toll of her choices remains unprocessed, and her path forward is solitary and undefined.
